title: The Flight of the Alone — Plotinus and the Interior Ascent Beyond Image
author: Plotinus / Stephen MacKenna

body.text: Plotinus begins with inward turning. He gives the inner architecture that theurgy depends on. The soul must withdraw from dispersion and rise toward the One. Where Iamblichus sacralizes the image, Plotinus teaches the moment beyond image.

Withdraw into yourself and look. And if you do not yet see yourself beautiful, do as does the maker of astatue that is to be made beautiful: he cuts away here, he smooths there, he makes this line lighter, this other purer, until alovely face has grown upon his work.

The self is astatue, unfinished. The work is removal — cutting away what obscures form until the hidden face appears. This is the inward equivalent of theurgy. The soul becomes its own statue. The divine form is revealed by subtraction.

body.caption: The soul as unfinished statue — purification as removal, not addition.

body.text: Never cease working at the statue until there shines out on you from it the godlike splendour of virtue, until you shall see the perfect goodness surely established in the stainless shrine.
The shrine is within. Virtue is luminosity — the soul made transparent enough for its higher form to shine. The soul is the shrine, but covered. The work renders it visible.
This is the life of gods and of godlike and blessed men: liberation from the alien that besets us here, alife taking no pleasure in the things of earth, the passing of solitary to solitary.

Solitary to solitary. The lower self is scattered across roles, fears, and identifications — aflake of dust dispersed across its own attention. The One can be reached only by abeing gathered to simplicity. The soul must become inwardly undivided. Then it may approach what is simple beyond it.

body.caption: Solitary to solitary — the soul gathered toward its source.

body.text: This is the last instruction. The One is beyond all formed content. Every image is aladder, and every ladder must eventually be released. The soul trains first through forms — beauty, virtue, symbol, intellect. Then it releases form into source.
This is the final re-rendering: from world as object, to world as symbol, to symbol as transparent, to the source beyond all rendering.
